shintowiki | Overview Shrine Ranking P11250 Run History | GitHub Wiki

Shintowiki Scripts

About the project

shinto.miraheze.org is an independent wiki documenting Shinto shrines, Japanese religious history, and related topics. It was founded after content created on English Wikipedia could no longer be maintained there, preserving thousands of articles about shrines, deities, and religious practices.

The wiki uses interlanguage link templates ({{ill}}) for every cross-wiki reference, and Wikidata integration ({{wikidata link}}) to connect pages to the linked data ecosystem. Every page is linked to its Wikidata item where one exists.

Wikidata QuickStatements

Shrine Ranking (P13723)

Add P459 determination method qualifiers to shrine ranking statements, P958 section qualifiers to Kokugakuin Museum entries, and migrate legacy properties.

Miraheze Article ID (P11250)

Link Wikidata items to their shintowiki articles. 0 pending statements.

Run History

Track daily QuickStatements submission outcomes — submitted, partial, skipped, or failed.

Automated maintenance

The shintowiki-scripts repository runs a daily GitHub Actions pipeline that performs automated maintenance on the wiki. All edits are made by User:EmmaBot with transparent edit summaries linking back to the workflow run.

Pipeline stages

  • Import & Categorization
  • Reimport pages from English Wikipedia
  • Create wanted categories as stubs
  • Triage autocreated categories (enwiki / jawiki / secondary)
  • Create shrine ranking article pages
  • Structural Fixes
  • Delete unused templates
  • Fix double redirects
  • Resolve duplicate QID disambiguation pages
  • Wikidata
  • Generate P11250 QuickStatements (view)
  • Clean completed QuickStatements
  • Tag pages without Wikidata links
  • Clean wikidata category redirects
  • Final
  • Fix template noinclude blocks
  • Categorize uncategorized pages
  • Tag untranslated Japanese content
  • Cleanup
  • Delete unused categories & broken redirects
  • Migrate & clean up talk pages
  • Remove crud category tags
